heaviest_rainfall.status: Today's storm ranked 6th for total rainfall with 20.31mm having fallen, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  5.4mm per hour fell at points during the storm which lasted 22.4 hours.  The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 47.89mm with a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: An active storm has been detected in the vicinity.  The last storm experienced was on 29 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 6 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwaves right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 18 July 2025, there have been only 62 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (200°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: To date, the maximum wind gust recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 6.8°C), outside temperature (up 6.1°C), and inside temperature (up 1.7°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 8%) and rainfall (up 1.8 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 490 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in rainfall (down 1.3 mm), cloud levels (up 315 m), feels like temperature (down 2.4°C), and outside temperature (down 2.2°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 489.7m lower than yesterday, 259.1m lower than the past week, 315.1m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 854m minimum and 1568m maximum, with an average of 626.2m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 885.4m and 941.4m.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mild and comfortable temperatures. Outside; prepare for cold, chilly weather ahead. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and winds will be practically absent. Moderate rainfall, low clouds are expected, possibly some fog or persistent clouds, and low sunlight due to cloudy or overcast skies. Changeable conditions.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (93%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
